issues.title: InputDecorator with UniqueKey prevents TextFormField child from getting focus
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files as follows: ... <!-- include every file that is different from the template app! -->
```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
        title: 'Flutter Demo',
        theme: ThemeData(
          primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
          visualDensity: VisualDensity.adaptivePlatformDensity,
        ),
        home: MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'));
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key key, this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  final _focusNodeOne = FocusNode();
  final _focusNodeTwo = FocusNode();

  @override
  void initState() {
    _focusNodeTwo.addListener(() {
      setState(() {});
    });
    super.initState();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: FocusTraversalGroup(
          child: Form(
            child: Column(
              children: [
                TextFormField(
                  decoration: const InputDecoration().copyWith(
                      border: OutlineInputBorder(), labelText: 'Field One'),
                  focusNode: _focusNodeOne,
                  onEditingComplete: () => _focusNodeTwo.requestFocus(),
                ),
                SizedBox(
                  height: 20,
                ),
                InputDecorator(
                  key: UniqueKey(), // <======================= Comment this out to make it work!
                  decoration: const InputDecoration().copyWith(
                      border: OutlineInputBorder(), labelText: 'Field Two'),
                  child: TextFormField(
                    decoration: const InputDecoration()
                        .copyWith(border: InputBorder.none),
                    focusNode: _focusNodeTwo,
                  ),
                  isFocused: _focusNodeTwo.hasFocus,
                ),
              ],
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
3. ... <!-- describe how to reproduce the problem -->
I have a form with two fields, and one of them is wrapped inside an `InputDecorator` instead of using its internal `decoration`.
Click on Field One. You can see that the cursor is blinking, and you can type straight away.
Now cycle to Field Two either by clicking or pressing tab (on flutter web). You'll notice that the cursor is not blinking as if inner `TextFormField` hasn't actually got the focus. A second click is required on Field Two to finally get the cursor ready to accept input.
The issue disappears if the `InputDecorator`'s `key: UniqueKey()` is commented out

I tried on both web and iOS

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
I would expect Field Two to be ready to accept input without having to click twice

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
The `InputDecorator` got the focus (as it's being repainted with highlighted border) but the inner `TextFormField` seems to have lost it as it won't accept any typed char unless you click a second time
